hero.title: Claude Code, mapped from the source.
hero.description: An English-first explorer for engineers who want the real shape of the reverse-engineered Claude Code repo: how the loop runs, where tools live, how memory persists, and which paths stay hidden behind feature gates.

featuredQuote: The most useful question is not “what does this repo claim to do?” It is “what actually happens after one prompt enters the loop?”

loopSteps.title: Shape the input
loopSteps.summary: User input is normalized, slash commands are intercepted, and session state is folded into the outgoing prompt.
loopSteps.detail: This is where raw terminal input stops being just text. The runtime can rewrite it, attach context, and divert slash commands before the model sees a single token.

loopSteps.title: Enter QueryEngine
loopSteps.summary: The session manager prepares prompt state, memory context, transcript persistence, and query options for one turn.
loopSteps.detail: QueryEngine is the session spine. It owns enough state to make retries, compaction, and persistence feel like one continuous conversation rather than disconnected API calls.

loopSteps.title: Stream the model turn
loopSteps.summary: The query loop streams assistant content, tool calls, and stop signals while deciding whether another pass is needed.
loopSteps.detail: The repo’s agentic feel comes from this loop. Tool calls, failures, retries, and budget logic all show up here as visible control flow rather than invisible magic.

loopSteps.title: Ground with tools
loopSteps.summary: Tool calls read files, search the repo, execute commands, or reach external systems before the answer continues.
loopSteps.detail: The runtime decides which capabilities exist for this turn. That filtering is what makes the answer evidence-backed instead of a clever but ungrounded guess.

loopSteps.title: Persist memory
loopSteps.summary: Session summaries and file-backed memories capture the useful part of the turn for later retrieval and consolidation.
loopSteps.detail: Memory is not treated as a giant external database here. It is a lightweight anti-forgetting layer that keeps the loop coherent across debugging, editing, and retries.

architecture.summary: The repo behaves like a compact operating system for coding work: terminal shell, orchestration, turn loop, tool execution, and supporting services.

categories.name: Terminal surface
categories.summary: Entrypoints, screens, and UI state compose the visible CLI shell before any model call happens.

categories.name: Orchestration core
categories.summary: QueryEngine, query.ts, and input processing coordinate one running session across many turns.

categories.name: Tool surface
categories.summary: The tool registry, tool definitions, and concrete tool implementations turn model intent into action.

categories.name: Memory & context
categories.summary: File-backed memory, retrieval, compaction, and attachment shaping keep long sessions usable.

categories.name: Services & extensions
categories.summary: Transport, analytics, plugins, skills, and commands extend the core loop into a full product surface.

tools.summary: The tool registry is where the runtime turns model intent into permissioned capability. It shows what the agent can really do in this environment.

memory.summary: This repo treats memory as a file-based execution aid, not a massive external knowledge graph. The goal is to keep useful context alive while the agent iterates.

highlights.title: Session transcripts act as episodic memory
highlights.summary: Every turn is persisted so the repo can reconstruct what happened, not just what the latest prompt says.

highlights.title: SessionMemory distills while the conversation is still live
highlights.summary: The runtime does lightweight distillation before the session ends, so useful structure is already present when the next turn starts.

highlights.title: extractMemories forks a sub-agent for semantic compression
highlights.summary: Persistent memories are promoted from dialogue into reusable Markdown files instead of staying trapped in transcript history.

highlights.title: autoDream consolidates, merges, and prunes
highlights.summary: Background consolidation keeps the file-based memory set from becoming a pile of repetitive fragments.

hiddenFeatures.name: KAIROS
hiddenFeatures.summary: Bundles assistant mode, background behavior, dream work, and user messaging flows.

hiddenFeatures.name: PROACTIVE
hiddenFeatures.summary: Allows the agent to wake itself up and decide whether it should act.

hiddenFeatures.name: COORDINATOR_MODE
hiddenFeatures.summary: Turns the main agent into a delegating coordinator for sub-agents.

hiddenFeatures.name: BRIDGE_MODE
hiddenFeatures.summary: Exposes a remote-control bridge for the CLI surface.

hiddenFeatures.name: EXTRACT_MEMORIES
hiddenFeatures.summary: Runs post-turn memory extraction through a forked sub-agent.

hiddenFeatures.name: TOKEN_BUDGET
hiddenFeatures.summary: Injects budget tracking and turn-level cost controls into the loop.

hiddenFeatures.name: BUDDY
hiddenFeatures.summary: Enables the mascot-style /buddy command and its terminal pet behavior.

hiddenFeatures.name: WORKFLOW_SCRIPTS
hiddenFeatures.summary: Turns packaged workflow scripts into callable commands and tools.
